meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Portfolio (Advanced)
content.seo_description: Bridge the 'no portfolio' gap by building a sophisticated SaaS pricing engine. Master real-world architecture using Antigravity and Claude Code today.
content.answer_block: To solve the no-portfolio dilemma, build a dynamic SaaS pricing engine. By integrating Stripe API, tiered subscription logic, and real-time usage tracking, you demonstrate high-level backend proficiency. This project showcases your ability to handle complex billing states and secure financial data, effectively turning a common technical hurdle into a professional asset.
content.intro:
You stare at a blank GitHub contribution graph, the silence of a non-existent portfolio echoing in your career prospects. The industry doesn't care about your GPA; they care about whether you can ship code that moves money. The 'no portfolio' trap is a psychological barrier, not a technical one, and the fastest way out is to build the one feature every startup founder is terrified of getting wrong: the pricing engine.

We aren't building a simple 'buy now' button. We are architecting a multi-tenant pricing model that handles webhooks, usage quotas, and subscription state transitions. Using the raw power of Antigravity and Claude Code, you will transform from a student into an engineer who understands the lifeblood of SaaS: recurring revenue architecture.

steps.title: Define the Tiered Architecture
steps.description: Establish a robust database schema using Prisma to support Free, Pro, and Enterprise tiers with varying feature flags and usage limits.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a relational schema for a multi-tenant SaaS pricing system. Include tables for Plans, Subscriptions, and UsageEvents. Ensure the schema supports feature-gating via JSONB columns.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create Prisma schema with Subscription and Plan models'

steps.title: Stripe Webhook Orchestration
steps.description: Implement a secure endpoint to handle Stripe events (subscription.created, invoice.payment_succeeded) to sync state with your database.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure Next.js API route to handle Stripe webhooks. Include signature verification, idempotency checks, and database updates for subscription status.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --command 'npm install stripe && touch app/api/webhooks/stripe/route.ts'

steps.title: Usage-Based Metering Logic
steps.description: Build an internal service that tracks user actions and throttles access based on plan limitations in real-time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Develop a middleware service that checks usage limits against the current subscription tier before allowing a resource-intensive API call.

steps.title: Dynamic Frontend Pricing Table
steps.description: Build a responsive pricing UI that reflects real-time plan status and handles the upgrade/downgrade flow via Stripe Checkout.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a React pricing component that fetches plan data from the backend and handles loading states during the Stripe checkout redirect.

steps.title: Testing Edge Cases
steps.description: Simulate failed payments and subscription cancellations to ensure your application remains stable and user-friendly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a test suite using Jest to simulate Stripe webhook failures and ensure the application gracefully handles subscription expiration.

pro_tips: Always implement idempotency keys when processing webhooks to prevent duplicate database updates during network retries.
Use 'stale-while-revalidate' caching for pricing tables to ensure users see updated plan details without hitting the DB on every page load.
Never store raw pricing logic in the frontend; always validate the subscription state on the server before executing protected actions.
Implement a 'Grace Period' logic in your database schema to prevent immediate service disruption on payment failure.
Document your webhook payload structures in your README; senior devs look for this to verify you understand data integrity.
content.next_steps: Deploy your application to Vercel, connect your custom domain, and write a 'Case Study' blog post explaining the architectural choices you made during this build.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped with the technical foundation to build production-grade financial systems. Stop waiting for permission to be an engineer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
